target.name: design-spec
target.source_summary: templates/design-spec.md + agents/designer.md (Strict Rules, Color System, Typography, Layout) + skills/design/build-ui.md

gates.description: The spec prescribes nothing on the banned list: frameworks/preprocessors/libraries, gradients, grey text, modals/popups/confirm(), fill icons, non-approved fonts or icon sources. A spec either prescribes a violation or it doesn't — no middle.
Typography names exactly one font family from the approved list (Inter, Archivo Narrow, DM Sans, Space Grotesk, Libre Franklin, Source Sans Pro) with 700/800 headings and 400 body.

dimensions.description: The direction is buildable without re-deciding anything visual.

anchors.1: Adjectives only ('clean, modern, professional') — no font name, no color values, nothing a builder could apply.
anchors.3: Font and brand color named, but the tinted-neutral derivations (--brand-accent, --contrast, --tint, --border-base, etc.) are unspecified.
anchors.5: Approved font plus the complete tinted-neutral palette with lightness targets per the build-ui table, plus a usage mapping (which variable for buttons, headings, backgrounds, borders).

dimensions.description: Flows use the prescribed interaction patterns.

anchors.1: Flows rely on modals, popups, or confirm() dialogs.
anchors.3: Mostly inline-expandable / dedicated-page patterns, but at least one flow is unclear or modal-shaped.
anchors.5: Small actions inline-expandable, large forms on dedicated pages, destructive actions inline are-you-sure with Yes/No — matching the pattern table exactly.

dimensions.description: Each key surface has an explicit hierarchy and the user's path through it is obvious.

anchors.1: Key surfaces listed without hierarchy — the primary action is not identifiable from the spec.
anchors.3: Hierarchy stated for main screens; loading/empty/edge states absent.
anchors.5: Each surface names primary content, secondary content, and navigation; flows between surfaces stated; loading and empty states designed.

dimensions.description: Header and navigation follow the system (100px header, app name left, email+dropdown right, two-tier nav).

anchors.3: Spec is silent on header/nav structure.
anchors.5: Header and two-tier navigation specified per the system, including active-page highlighting.

dimensions.description: Implementation notes let a builder start without re-deciding.

anchors.1: Section absent or restates the visual direction.
anchors.3: CSS variable guidance present; file organization (global styles.css vs page-specific) unaddressed.
anchors.5: Variables, file structure, asset guidance, and responsive expectations stated — buildable as written.

dimensions.description: The design has an intentional, recognizable direction tied to the product's purpose — not default-framework aesthetics. Deliberately low-weighted: it is the most subjective dimension, and an unanchored quality dimension is the biggest source of noise.

anchors.1: Indistinguishable from a default component-library look; no stated reason for any choice.
anchors.3: Coherent and compliant but safe — choices are defensible, none are motivated by this product specifically.
anchors.5: A recognizable point of view: the spec says why this palette/type/density fits this product and audience, and the choices visibly follow from it.

aggregation.gate_handling: Apply gate caps after normalization; lowest applicable cap wins.

weight_rationale: visual_direction_specificity is heaviest because a generic direction is precisely the 'bland framework-looking UI' the designer Must Not ship, and vagueness here forces every downstream visual decision to be re-made ad hoc.

unmeasurable_rules.rule: Match an exemplar only when the task explicitly requires it
unmeasurable_rules.home: Partially visible in the artifact (unexplained mimicry); behavioral side has no v1 surface — no fetch tool exists. Rule remains prompt-enforced; flagged, not graded.

known_good.summary: Dashboard design spec with full token system.
known_good.content: Intent: ops dashboard for delivery monitoring — calm, scannable, status-first because users glance 20x/day. Typography: Space Grotesk only; 800 headings / 400 body. Color: --brand #1D6E5A; --brand-accent 95%L; --brand-alt 82%L; --contrast 12%L; --tint 98%L; --border-base 88%L; mapping: brand=primary buttons/links, contrast=text, tint=section bg. Surfaces: Deliveries list (primary: status table; secondary: filters; nav: two-tier, header 100px, email dropdown right); empty state: 'no deliveries yet' + create action; loading: text-only indicator. Interactions: row expands inline for judgments; approvals on dedicated page; abandon = inline Are-you-sure Yes/No. Implementation: global styles.css with variables above; no page CSS yet; icons Remix line-only with labels.

known_bad.summary: Vibes spec prescribing a modal and a gradient.
known_bad.content: Intent: make it feel modern and clean. Visual direction: use a nice sans-serif, maybe Inter or Roboto; primary color blue with a subtle gradient header. Key surfaces: main page, settings. Interaction: confirmation modal before abandoning a delivery. Implementation notes: use Tailwind for speed.
